patch.value: CodeFactor remediation: drive code quality from A to A+ (zero outstanding findings)

Track and eliminate every CodeFactor finding so the repository reaches the best attainable rating with no outstanding issues.

## Source

- CodeFactor overview: https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/overview/main
- Issues: https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/main
- Files by rating: https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/files/main?grid-column=Rating&grid-dir=0

CodeFactor was wired up alongside Codecov in commit `d42ae71b` (README badge + `.codefactor.yml` scoping analysis to hand-authored source, excluding `.agents/**`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `PRD.md`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`).

## Snapshot (captured 2026-06-26)

- Overall grade: **A** (98.7% A-rated files, 1.0% B, 0.3% C)
- Files analyzed: 601 · Active branch: main
- **Total outstanding issues: 261**

| Category | Count | Severity / rule split |
|---|---|---|
| Complexity | 152 | Critical 3 · High 7 · Moderate 142 |
| Maintainability | 89 | `no-useless-fallback-in-spread` 57 · unsafe optional chaining 25 · `no-thenable` 4 · `no-useless-spread` 2 · `no-useless-length-check` 1 |
| Duplication | 20 | Critical 2 · Major 3 · Minor 15 |

## Hotspot files (lowest-rated)

1. `src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts` (B) — includes the `no-thenable` findings (`then:` keys at lines 2483, 2493)
2. `tests/unit/commands/create-command.spec.ts` (C)
3. `tests/unit/commands/update-command.spec.ts` (B)
4. `src/cli/commands/search.ts` (B)
5. `src/cli/main.ts` (B)

## Approach

Child items break the work down by category and rule. Order of attack:
1. Maintainability — mechanical, low-risk, auto-fixable ESLint rules (fastest grade wins).
2. Duplication — extract shared helpers (Critical/Major first).
3. Complexity — refactor the worst functions (Critical/High first), then chip away at Moderate.
4. Stand up local lint/quality parity so the team can reach zero and prevent regressions.

NOTE: CodeFactor analyzes its own snapshot; `.agents/**` (the git-native work-item store, where these very items live), `CHANGELOG.md`, `PRD.md`, and the lockfile are excluded by `.codefactor.yml`, so these tracking items do not themselves add findings.

CodeFactor overall repository grade reaches A+ (or the highest attainable) on branch main; CodeFactor reports 0 outstanding issues across Complexity, Maintainability, and Duplication; local lint/quality parity tooling exists so findings can be reproduced and kept at zero (no regressions on new PRs).

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Progress update: live CodeFactor for main still reports 255 issues until this branch lands (83 maintainability, 152 complexity, 20 duplication). This branch completes the maintainability groups tracked by pm-xsth, pm-m8yl, and pm-x878/Codecov; complexity pm-arzz and duplication pm-fmjy remain open for dedicated refactor follow-up.

value.author: codex-codefactor-sdk-cli-20260626
value.text: Investigation note: live 2026-06-26 CodeFactor public issues page reports A rating, 601 analyzed files, 172 issues after the merged maintainability branch; PM snapshot still tracks remaining complexity and duplication children. GitHub state: no open PRs, no open Dependabot or code-scanning alerts; issue #374 remains open and externally blocked on missing RELEASE_PAT in release environment; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate reports no known vulnerabilities. Dependency drift from pnpm outdated: @sentry/cli 3.5.1 -> 3.6.0, @sentry/node 10.61.0 -> 10.62.0, @types/node held at Node 22 types despite latest 26.x.

value.author: codex-codefactor-sdk-cli-20260626
value.text: Evidence update: CodeFactor remediation bundle now covers the remaining duplication primitives and generated/test analysis scope. Verification passed: pnpm build; targeted 254-test suite; pnpm contracts:check; pnpm quality:static; pnpm typecheck; pnpm security:scan; full coverage 100/100/100/100 across 5133 tests; manual isolated temp-project smoke with PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH passed package install/doctor and shared unset behavior. Remaining release issue #374 is external to code changes: GitHub scheduled Auto Release requires RELEASE_PAT in the release environment.

value.note: pm-changelog package-owned check refreshed managed extension install timestamp

value.author: codex-codefactor-sdk-cli-20260626
value.text: Changelog evidence: pnpm changelog:pm:check installed pm-changelog 2026.6.20, activated changelog export/generate commands, and reported CHANGELOG.md unchanged with item_count=1353 and bytes=217874.

value.author: codex-codefactor-sdk-cli-20260626
value.text: Observability evidence: sentry issue list returned recent unresolved CLI issue records with scrubbed paths and useful short IDs/permalinks; sentry-telemetry-gate passed for unbrained/pm-cli with critical=0, high=0, access_ok=true, token_source=SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN, and telemetry finish_error_rate_pct=0.66 with failures_without_error_code_rows=0. pm telemetry stats showed 13 queued rows, 0 invalid rows, recent health/stats finish events, and 0 local error_rate for sampled finish rows.

value.author: codex-codefactor-sdk-cli-20260626
value.text: Review response: addressed Sourcery/Gemini feedback on PR #380 by replacing UPDATE_UNSET_FIELD_DEFINITIONS slice-based insertion with named shared groups around close-reason and documenting why core mutation option keys must stay aligned with CLI unset metadata without importing CLI modules into core. Verification after review fix passed pnpm build, pnpm quality:static, pnpm contracts:check, and targeted create/update/SDK tests (3 files, 313 tests).

value.author: codex-codefactor-sdk-cli-20260626
value.text: Merge evidence: PR #380 merged into main as e89d1e24 after second review loop. Current-head checks were green: CodeFactor, CodeQL, CodeRabbit, Sourcery, cubic, Hound, Docs and Skills, Build foundation, Gates coverage/static/typecheck/compat/smokes, Runtime smoke Ubuntu Node 22/24, macOS Node 24, Windows regression Node 24, and codecov/patch. Stale Gemini slice thread was outdated and resolved after 4acf9af9 replaced slice ordering with named groups.
